# Storage Fixtures — Block Devices, Partitions, Filesystems

Captured output for the **Storage Adapter** (`bcs.platform.adapters.storage`,
see [docs/STORAGE_ADAPTER.md](../../../../docs/STORAGE_ADAPTER.md)). General
collection, locale, anonymization, naming, and placeholder rules are in the
[corpus root README](../README.md) and are not restated here.

## Required Command Lines

Exactly the three invocations the adapter's accepted design specifies — no
other flags ([docs/STORAGE_ADAPTER.md § Linux Storage
Tools](../../../../docs/STORAGE_ADAPTER.md#linux-storage-tools)):

```
LC_ALL=C LANG=C lsblk -J -b   > lsblk_<version>_<platform>_<scenario>.txt
LC_ALL=C LANG=C blkid -p -o json > blkid_<version>_<platform>_<scenario>.txt
LC_ALL=C LANG=C findmnt -J    > findmnt_<version>_<platform>_<scenario>.txt
```

Each scenario is a **triple** — one file per tool, all captured from the same
system state at the same time, sharing the same `<scenario>` field, so
cross-referencing tests can verify correct merging. Record each tool's own
version (`lsblk --version`, `blkid --version`, `findmnt --version`) in the
inventory table below and in the corresponding filename.

## Layout

No vendor subdirectories, unlike `firmware/`. `lsblk`/`blkid`/`findmnt`
output is generated by generic Linux userspace tools reading standardized
block-device and filesystem metadata — it is not firmware-vendor-specific
the way `efibootmgr`'s device-path text can be, so there is no equivalent
per-OEM quirk this corpus needs to capture separately. All fixtures live
directly under this directory.

## Inventory

Every fixture (including placeholders) gets a row here. **All current
entries are zero-byte placeholders** — no real output has been captured yet;
see the root README's placeholder rules.

| File | Scenario | Status | Tool version | Captured on / from | Anonymized |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lsblk_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_nvme-laptop-esp-root.txt` | Typical NVMe laptop: single NVMe disk, GPT+ESP, one root partition | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `blkid_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_nvme-laptop-esp-root.txt` | (same scenario, `blkid` half of the triple) | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `findmnt_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_nvme-laptop-esp-root.txt` | (same scenario, `findmnt` half of the triple) | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `lsblk_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_classroom-esp-root-home-swap.txt` | Classroom machine: NVMe disk, ESP + root + home + swap | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `blkid_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_classroom-esp-root-home-swap.txt` | (same scenario, `blkid` half of the triple) | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `findmnt_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_classroom-esp-root-home-swap.txt` | (same scenario, `findmnt` half of the triple) | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `lsblk_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_usb-recovery-drive.txt` | USB recovery drive: removable disk, single vfat partition | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `blkid_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_usb-recovery-drive.txt` | (same scenario, `blkid` half of the triple) | placeholder | — | — | — |
| `findmnt_unknown_ubuntu-24.04_usb-recovery-drive.txt` | (same scenario, `findmnt` half of the triple) | placeholder | — | — | — |

These three scenarios mirror the parser test plan in
[docs/STORAGE_ADAPTER.md § Testing Strategy](../../../../docs/STORAGE_ADAPTER.md#testing-strategy).
A fourth scenario category from that plan, malformed/truncated tool output,
is deliberately **not** represented here: malformed output is not something
a healthy real system produces to capture, so those cases are covered only
by the synthetic, `tmp_path`-rooted corpus inside
`cli/tests/test_platform_adapters_storage_parser.py` — the same treatment
the EFI Adapter's own malformed-line scenarios received.
